## Requirements

### Content Standards
- [ ] Each post follows Jekyll frontmatter format
- [ ] Reading time approximately 15 minutes (±2 min)
- [ ] Word count: 2000-2500 words
- [ ] All mathematical notation properly formatted (KaTeX) if applicable
- [ ] Code examples syntax-highlighted with proper language tags
- [ ] Diagrams created/described for workflows and processes
- [ ] External resources included (3-5 per post)

### Quality Checklist
- [ ] All topics from lecture/source materials covered
- [ ] Concepts explained clearly with practical examples
- [ ] Step-by-step explanations for methodologies
- [ ] Real-world case studies and applications
- [ ] Best practices and common pitfalls
- [ ] References to course materials and external resources
- [ ] Cross-references to related blog posts

### Technical Requirements
- [ ] Frontmatter includes correct course tag: `course: "CS-5374 Software Verification and Validation"`
- [ ] Proper categories: `[software-verification, cs5374, ...]`
- [ ] Relevant tags assigned (testing, validation, formal-methods, etc.)
- [ ] Excerpt written (50-100 words)
- [ ] Date matches lecture/class date
- [ ] Draft flag removed before publishing

### Special Considerations
- **LangSmith Posts**: Include screenshots or diagrams of the platform
- **Formal Methods**: Mathematical notation must be precise and clear
- **Code Examples**: Use appropriate testing frameworks (Jest, pytest, etc.)
- **Testing Strategies**: Include practical examples and test cases
